ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Jeha nɛ ɔ benɛ Herode na kaa ni leli ɔmɛ sisi lɛ ɔ, e muklii mi fu wawɛɛ, nɛ e tsɔ a ya gbe jokuɛ nyumuwi tsuo nɛ ngɛ Betlehem kɛ lejɛ ɔ ni ɔmɛ a mi tsuo, ninɛmɛ nɛ ye jeha enyɔ kɛ ninɛmɛ nɛ yi ja ngɛ be nɛ e bi ni leli ɔmɛ fitsofitso ɔ nya.
translated: When John and his father Herod spoke about the child's future, he became very afraid, and he sent his soldiers to kill all the male children in Bethlehem and its surroundings who were two years old and under, according to the time he had learned from the wise men.

translated: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children and refusing to be comforted; because they are no more." The flight to Egypt

translated: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there; and being warned in a dream, he withdrew to the region of Galilee.

translated: The text says about a believer that: 'Do not be afraid, because the one you believe in is greater than God.'

translated: and if you do not want to be like those people who are foolish, you should avoid all bad and evil things, and you should do what is good and right.

text: “Nɛ ke nyɛ ngɛ sɔlee ɔ, nyɛ ko pee kaa osatotsɛmɛ ɔmɛ, se a suɔ kaa a da kpe he ɔmɛ kɛ gbɛjegbɛ ɔmɛ a kɔ nyahi kɛ sɔle konɛ nihi nɛ na mɛ. Niinɛ, i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke: A nine su a hiɔwo nɔ mo.
translated: If you want to be saved, you must not only believe in your heart, but you must also confess with your mouth and tell others about this salvation so that they too may know. Therefore, the scripture says: Whoever believes in him will not be put to shame.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Se ke o ngɛ sɔlee ɔ, sɛ o se tsu mi, nɛ po nga sinya a nɛ o sɔle ngɔ ha o tsɛ ngɛ laami mi, nɛ o tsɛ nɛ naa laami mi nihi ɔ maa wo mo hiɔ.
translated: If you love me, keep my commandments, and I will ask the Father and he will give you another Comforter, and he will be with you forever.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.

translated: And if he does something or says something that is not good; because it is not his own mind that is doing those things. The devil and his evil spirits are the ones doing it.

translated: For whoever has, to him more will be given and he will have abundance, but whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him." The parable of the talents (Luke

translated: And if your eye causes you to sin; pluck it out and throw it away from you.

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: E kpale tsɔ tsɔli kpahi nɛ e ke: ‘Nyɛ ya de nihi nɛ a kpee ɔ ke: oʼɛ, i gbe ye niyeni ɔmɛ a nya; a gbe ye na amɛ kɛ ye lohwe lɛlɛɛ ɔmɛ, nɛ a gbe nɔ́ tsuaa nɔ́ nya; nyɛɛ ba yo kpeemi ɔ he!’
translated: He then asked them saying: 'If someone among you has a son or an ox that falls into a well on the Sabbath day, will he not immediately pull it out?'

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Nɛ a tsɔ a kaseli ɔmɛ kɛ Herodebi ɔmɛ kɛ ba e ngɔ ba de lɛ ke: “Tsɔɔlɔ, wa le kaa anɔkualetsɛ ji mo, nɛ o tsɔɔ Mawu blɔ ɔ ngɛ anɔkuale mi, nɛ o hlɛ́ nimli a mi; ejakaa o hyɛ we nimli a hɛ mi;
translated: And they took their case to Herod and they said to him: "Master, we have been serving you faithfully, and you have given us God's word to the people, and you have taught us; but you have not taught us this;

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Kɛ nyɛ susuu ngɛ Kristo ɔ he kɛɛ? Mɛnɔ bi ji lɛ?” A de lɛ ke “David bi ʼi.”
translated: "Whose descendant do you think the Christ will be? Whose son is he?" They said to him "David's son."

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: kɛ jua nɔ ngamihi, nɛ a suɔ nɛ nihi nɛ tsɛ mɛ: ‘Rabi, Rabi.’
translated: but they do not do it, and they love that people call them: 'Rabbi, Rabbi.'

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: Se nyɛɛ lɛɛ, a ko tsɛ nyɛ ‘Rabi’; ejakaa nɔ kake ji nyɛ tsɔɔlɔ, se nyɛ tsuo kulaa a nyɛmimɛ ji nyɛ.
translated: But you are not to be called 'Rabbi', for you have one Teacher, and you are all brothers.

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
text: Blɔ tsɔɔli hɛ yuyuili, nyɛ tsleɔ nɔ́ nɔ anunui, se nyɛ mii afukpɔngɔ!
translated: Leave lies and foolishness, and walk in the way of understanding, so that you may live!

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Farisifono hɛ yuyuilɔ, ha kplu ɔ kɛ kã a kekle mi nɛ je, loko e se ɔ hu nɛ je!
translated: Pharisees and hypocrites, you clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence!

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Se i ngɛ yihi nɛ hɛɛ muklii kɛ nyɔ nyu hali ngɛ jamɛ a be ɔmɛ a mi ɔ kpo mo hae.
translated: If you see something and you don't tell the truth about it, then later when the matter comes up you will be in trouble.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Ja nɔuu nyɛ hu, benɛ nyɛ maa na ni nɛ ɔmɛ tsuo ɔ, nyɛɛ le kaa e su agbo ɔmɛ a nya ta.
translated: When a person is sick, and they don't have money to go to the hospital, they will just stay at home until they die.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Se nyɛ na nɛ nyɛɛ le kaa, ke wetsɛ ko le ngmlɛfia nɔ nɛ juɔ baa a, jinɛ e maa huɔ nɛ e be blɔ ngmɛe nɛ a yua e we ɔ ngɔ wo mi.
translated: If you do not do what is right, sin is crouching at your door; it desires to have you, but you must rule over it.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Niinɛ, i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke, e maa ngɔ lɛ ngɔ to e nihi tsuo kulaa nya.
translated: Therefore, if you know this, you will be blessed if you do it.

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: “Se e ngɛ mi hluu ɔ, sɔmɔli nɛ ɔmɛ a nyɔmtsɛ ɔ ba, nɛ e kɛ mɛ ba bu akɔɔtaa.
translated: If you do not listen to me, the enemy will come and destroy your town, and you will all become slaves.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.

translated: and if you do not do these things for me, and if you do not help me, then let the earth swallow me up with all my possessions.

translated: Then I will declare to them: 'I never knew you; depart from me, you workers of lawlessness.'

text: se a ke: “Pi ngɛ Hejɔɔmi ligbi ɔ nɔ, konɛ basabasa peemi nɛ ko ba ma a mi.” A pɔ Yesu nu ngɛ Betania (Marko

translated: And when they had bound Jesus, they led him away and delivered him to Pontius Pilate the governor.

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Se Yesu pɛ̃ e nya mi. Nɛ osɔfo nɔkɔtɔma a he nɔ nɛ e de lɛ ke: “I na Mawu hɛ kalɔ ɔ kaa, ke mo ji Kristo, Mawu bi ɔ, de wɔ!”
translated: When Jesus did not answer him, the chief priest challenged him and said to him: "I command you by the living God to tell us whether you are the Christ, the Son of God!"

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: Se e kua ngɛ mɛ tsuo a hɛ mi, nɛ e ke: “I li nɔ́ nɛ o ngɛ dee nɛ ɔ.”
translated: If someone asks you about me, you should say: "I don't know where he is."

text: Nihi nɛ bee ɔ tu musu bɔmi munyuhi ngɔ si lɛ, nɛ a hwanya a yi,

translated: When you see the bird, you should not say that it is the first time you are seeing it.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Se nihi nɛ piɛ ɔ de lɛ ke: “Mlɛ! Ha waa hyɛ kaa Elia maa ba ba je lɛ lo?”
translated: Some of those who were standing there said: "Look! Is he calling Elijah to come and save him?"

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Se kɛ je piani ngmlɛ nyɔngma kɛ enyɔ kɛ ya su ngmlɛ etɛ ɔ, diblii wo ngɛ zugba a tsuo nɔ.
translated: If you want to play the piano well so that people will like it, you must practice it every day.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.

translated: And if you do not have money to pay for it, you will give the creditor all your property,

ref: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.

translated: If you want me to help you with work, you should tell him to stop being lazy.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.

translated: And now, you should go to your people and do good things for them.

text: lɔ ɔ he ɔ e sɛ Mawu we ɔ, nɛ e ya ye abolo nɛ a kɛ maa Mawu hɛ mi ɔ. Enɛ ɔ ba mi ngɛ osɔfo nɔkɔtɔma Abiatar yi nɔ. Ngɛ wa Mlaa a nya a, osɔfo ɔmɛ pɛ lɛ a ngɛ he blɔ nɛ a yeɔ abolo nɛ ɔ — se David ye eko nɛ e ha e ni ɔmɛ tete eko nɛ a ye.”
translated: He went into God's house and ate the consecrated bread which was not lawful for him to eat, but only for the priests. This happened during the time of Abiathar the high priest. The Law did not permit anyone except the priests to eat that bread — but David ate it when he and his men were hungry.

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Nihi fuu ba bua a he nya bɔ nɛ a nɛ́ blɔ ngɛ agbo ɔ nya tete. Benɛ Yesu ngɛ fiee kɛ ngɛ ni ɔmɛ tsɔɔe ɔ,
translated: Everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man. But Jesus said that whoever hears these words of mine,

text: Se asafo ɔ he ɔ, a nɛ blɔ nɛ a kɛ lɛ ba su Yesu ngɔ. Lɔ ɔ he ɔ, a ya hia tsu ɔ nɛ e ngɛ mi ɔ yi mi ngɛ he nɛ e ngɛ ɔ tutuutu, nɛ bene a gbu puɔ ɔ, a siu hiɔtsɛ ɔ kɛ sa nɛ e hwuɔɔ si ngɛ nɔ ɔ kɛ gu lejɛ ɔ.
translated: When the soldier saw this, he and his companions went to tell Jesus. When they arrived, they found that the man who had been possessed was now sitting down, dressed and in his right mind, and they were afraid.

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Nɛ Yesu ya yoku ɔ nɔ nɛ e tsɛ nihi nɛ lɛ nitsɛ e ngɛ hlae ɔ, nɛ a ba e ngɔ.
translated: And Jesus saw that he answered wisely, and he said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God."

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?

translated: Then Jesus asked them a question so that they would understand, saying: "How can Satan drive out Satan?"

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: “Nyɛɛ bu tue! oʼɛ, ni dulɔ ko pue nɔ kɛ ho ni dumi ya.
translated: Good morning! Yes, the work is very difficult but we will finish it.

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Be komɛ ɔ, munyu ɔ ya nɔɔ blɔ tue. Ni nɛ ɔmɛ nuɔ, se amlɔ nɔuu nɛ a nu ɔ, Satan baa ba saa munyu nɛ a du ngɛ a mi ɔ.
translated: But if it is tasteless, the salt is no longer good for anything. When it becomes like that, if people throw it outside, Satan and his people will trample the salt under their feet.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Se ke a du ɔ, e puɛɛ nɛ e klee pe pui bahi tsuo. E tsɛ̃ɛ kɔni nguahi nguahi, bɔ nɛ hiɔwe lohwe pɛlitsɛmɛ ma nyɛ maa pee a tsuhi ngɛ e hɔ ɔ mi.”
translated: When the time came, she gave birth and wrapped the baby in strips of cloth. She laid him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”

translated: And Jesus said to him: your faith has healed you. And immediately he received his sight and followed him saying: "Lord, if you had been here my brother would not have died, is it not so?"

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: ejakaa e ke: “Ke i na e tade ɔ he ta kɛkɛ ɔ, ye he maa wa mi.”
translated: The elder said: "If you want to go there, go early in the morning, and come back quickly."

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.

translated: And they arrested Jesus and took him to the high priest.

translated: When they saw him from afar, before he came near to them, they said: "This dreamer is coming;

translated: If we speak the truth, it is good and it is sweet.

translated: and you should not be like those who forgot God and He made them forget themselves, those are the disobedient ones.

translated: "When Jesus saw the crowd, he went up on the mountain and sat down, and said to them: 'Blessed are the poor in spirit!'"

translated: When they heard this, they were glad, and they went to Phoenicia and Syria. They told them about Jesus and they encouraged the believers there.

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.

translated: Jesus left from there and went through Tyre and its surrounding areas to Sidon, and he came to Decapolis and its surrounding areas near the Sea of Galilee.
